title: component-embed renders real iframe pointing to latest artifact
description: Upgrade the `component-embed` NodeView (placeholder built in TASK-29) to render a real `<iframe>` sourced from the latest published preview artifact for its `componentName`/`variantId`, when both are set.
acceptanceCriteria: When a `component-embed` node has non-empty `componentName` and `variantId`, its NodeView renders an `<iframe>` with `src` pointing at the latest artifact's static path (via TASK-46's serving route), resolved through a `componentPreviews.getLatest` query
Iframe has a sensible default height/width with a resize affordance or auto-height behavior appropriate for typical component previews
If no published preview exists yet for the referenced component/variant, the placeholder empty-state from TASK-29 is shown instead of a broken iframe (full behavior detailed in TASK-51)

Iframe uses `sandbox` attributes appropriate for running untrusted-ish third-party component code safely within the admin panel (e.g. `allow-scripts` but not `allow-same-origin` combined carelessly — document the chosen sandbox policy)

steps.details: Add `componentPreviews: { getLatest: protectedProcedure.input(z.object({ componentName: z.string(), variantId: z.string() })).query(...) }` to a new `apps/server/src/trpc/routers/componentPreviews.ts`, using TASK-42's `getLatestPreview` helper and resolving it to a servable URL via TASK-46's route scheme.

In `apps/admin/src/features/editor/nodes/ComponentEmbed.ts`'s React NodeView, when `componentName`/`variantId` are set, call `componentPreviews.getLatest` and render an `<iframe src={result.url} sandbox="allow-scripts" />` on success.

If `getLatest` returns null (no artifact published for this component/variant), fall back to the TASK-29 placeholder box with an updated message indicating the component is referenced but not yet published (full empty-state polish in TASK-51).

Give the iframe a reasonable default height (e.g. 240px) with a manual resize handle, or use a `postMessage`-based auto-resize if `preview-kit` is extended to report its content height (optional enhancement, minimum requirement is a usable fixed/resizable default).

Add a code comment explaining exactly which `sandbox` tokens are enabled and why (e.g. `allow-scripts` needed to run the component; `allow-same-origin` deliberately omitted unless required by a specific feature, to limit the iframe's ability to read/write the parent's cookies).

Using Playwright: with a real published preview artifact (from an end-to-end TASK-41→TASK-43 flow), insert a component-embed referencing it and confirm the iframe renders the component visually; reference a component with no published artifact and confirm the empty-state placeholder shows instead.

technicalNotes: This task deliberately reuses TASK-29's node/NodeView structure and only changes the rendering logic — the persisted JSON shape (`componentName`, `variantId`) is unchanged, so content authored in Fase 3 remains valid.
